meta.name: Dhruva
meta.tagline: Your AI. Your phone. Nobody else's business.
meta.story: Dhruva (ध्रुव) is the pole star: the one point in a turning sky that never moves, granted to a boy whose resolve did not need an audience or a network to hold steady — this app is that fixed point for AI, running entirely on your device, visible only to you.

_notes.outline: Non-text UI boundary color (dividers, input borders). Verified at >=3:1 against both surface and background, the applicable threshold for non-text UI components, not the 4.5:1 text threshold.
_notes.outlineVariant: Deliberately BELOW the 3:1 bar (1.65:1 vs surface) — per Material 3's own spec, outlineVariant is a decorative/low-emphasis divider explicitly exempt from contrast minimums, one step quieter than outline. Not a bug.
_notes.onPrimary: Reuses the background hex deliberately: the pole star's light against the night it sits in.
_notes.inversePrimary: 4.36:1 against inverseSurface — passes the large-text/UI-component bar, not the 4.5:1 body-text bar. Inverse surfaces (snackbars, tooltips) are short-lived and typically large/bold text; flagged here rather than silently eyeballed.
_notes.scrim: Functional modal/backdrop overlay applied at partial opacity (recommended ~48-60% alpha), not a fixed foreground/background pair — no single contrast ratio applies. Uses midnight.900 instead of pure black to stay tinted to the brand hue.

_notes.outline: Non-text UI boundary color. Verified at >=3:1 against both surface and background.
_notes.outlineVariant: Deliberately BELOW the 3:1 bar (1.51:1 vs surface) — decorative/low-emphasis divider, exempt from contrast minimums per Material 3 spec, quieter than outline on purpose.
_notes.inversePrimary: 8.30:1 against inverseSurface — clears the full text bar, not just large-text.
_notes.scrim: Functional overlay at partial opacity, same midnight.900 base as the dark theme (scrim doesn't invert with theme — it's always the darkest, most neutral tone available).
_notes.designIntent: Light theme is dawn-after-the-vigil, not a default afterthought: warm bronze-gold primary (deep enough to read as text at 5.58:1), deep polaris-blue secondary, same story-derived hues as dark, just recalibrated for a lit sky instead of a dark one.

typography.note: Family names are Google Fonts identifiers, usable directly via the google_fonts Flutter package and Google Fonts CDN/self-host on the Astro site. Each role lists a devanagariFallback for Hindi (hi) locale strings; the app's font-resolution order for hi is [role.family, role.devanagariFallback, system-ui]. heightMultiplier = lineHeight / size (3 decimals) — feed this directly into Flutter's TextStyle.height, which is a unitless multiplier, not a px value; the px lineHeight stays for the web/CSS consumer.

display.role: Editorial serif carrying the myth: display, headline, and titleLarge.

ui.role: Humanist geometric sans for interface text: titleMedium/Small, body, label.

elevation.note: Dark theme (default/hero) avoids literal drop shadows — a night sky doesn't cast hard shadows. Elevation reads as tonal lift: each level blends a small percentage of primary (starGold) into the surface color instead of adding a shadow. Light theme uses conventional soft shadows tinted toward midnight-500, never neutral gray.

motion.philosophy: The pole star does not rush to prove it's there. Entrances settle rather than bounce; nothing overshoots or wobbles.

easing.banned: No bounce, elastic, or spring-overshoot curves anywhere in the app.

iconography.set: Phosphor Icons (regular weight for default UI, bold weight reserved for active/selected states only)
iconography.strokeWeight: Regular weight approximates a 1.5px stroke at 24px grid; never mix weights within one screen
iconography.cornerStyle: Rounded caps and joins, matching the softened-serif warmth of Fraunces rather than a sharp technical grid
iconography.motif: A single recurring accent glyph across the app: a 4-point star (see brand-proposal.md logo mark) used as the loading/thinking indicator, the empty-state mark, and the unread/active dot — never as decoration without semantic meaning
iconography.avoid: Default Material Icons wholesale, generic circular spinners, gradient icon fills
